appeal_text: We respectfully request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki (Enhertu, CPT 96413) for our patient with HER2-low metastatic breast cancer (ICD-10 C50.911). Per the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage (Eff. April 1, 2026), coverage of non-preferred oncology products requires documented intolerance or contraindication to a preferred product and physician attestation that the same adverse event would not be expected with the requested agent. The submitted record includes: (1) prior pertuzumab plus trastuzumab plus taxane regimen 2025-02 through 2025-08 with documented progression on imaging 2025-09-14; (2) prior T-DM1 (ado-trastuzumab emtansine) initiated 2025-10-02, discontinued 2025-12-18 for grade-3 thrombocytopenia recurring after dose reduction; (3) HER2 IHC 2+, ISH ratio 1.4, classifying tumor as HER2-low and qualifying patient under the DESTINY-Breast04 indication on the FDA-approved Enhertu prescribing information. The NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Breast Cancer V.1.2026 list fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki as a Category 1 preferred regimen for HER2-low metastatic breast cancer after one or more prior chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting; this is also listed in the NCCN Drugs & Biologics Compendium. The patient's documented T-DM1 thrombocytopenia is a specific contraindication addressed by the policy's intolerance exception. We request peer-to-peer review with a UHC oncology medical director within five business days. Internal appeal is submitted within the 65-day timely-filing window. Attached: original denial letter, pathology report, infusion records, and CBC trend.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Breast Cancer V.1.2026
NCCN Drugs & Biologics Compendium
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Enhertu (fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of single-agent pembrolizumab (Keytruda, CPT 96365) as first-line therapy for our patient with metastatic non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer (ICD-10 C34.91). The denial cited PD-L1 TPS of 35% and missing EGFR/ALK testing. Subsequent to the denial we have completed comprehensive genomic profiling. Updated documentation: (1) repeat IHC at our institutional reference lab reports PD-L1 TPS 60% (CPS 65), exceeding the ≥50% threshold required by the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage; (2) FoundationOne CDx reports no actionable driver alterations including EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MET exon 14, RET, NTRK, KRAS G12C, HER2; (3) the patient is ECOG 1, weight 71 kg, with normal hepatic and renal function. Per NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer V.5.2026, single-agent pembrolizumab is a Category 1 preferred regimen for metastatic NSCLC with PD-L1 TPS ≥50% and no contraindications to immunotherapy. The FDA-approved Keytruda label supports this indication on the basis of KEYNOTE-024. The corrected biomarker results resolve the substantive basis for denial. Internal appeal is submitted within the 65-day timely-filing window. We request peer-to-peer review with a thoracic medical oncologist. Attached: updated pathology report, NGS report, ECOG documentation, and the original denial letter.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer V.5.2026
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Keytruda (pembrolizumab)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of intensity-modulated radiation therapy delivery (CPT 77386) for our patient with intermediate-risk prostate adenocarcinoma (ICD-10 C61, Gleason 4+3=7, PSA 12.4, T2c). Per the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy, IMRT for prostate cancer is proven and medically necessary when 3D-conformal planning cannot achieve organ-at-risk dose constraints or when dose escalation requires improved conformality. Attached comparative dosimetry demonstrates: (1) the 3D-conformal plan exceeds the rectal V70 constraint (24% vs the institutional limit of 15%) and the bladder V65 constraint (38% vs 25%); (2) the IMRT plan reduces rectal V70 to 11% and bladder V65 to 19%; (3) PTV D95 dose escalation to 79.2 Gy is required by the NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Prostate Cancer for intermediate-risk disease. ASTRO Model Policy on IMRT recognizes prostate cancer as a covered indication. The dosimetric comparison directly satisfies the UHC policy criterion. Submitted within the 65-day timely-filing window. Attached: 3D-conformal plan, IMRT plan, DVH overlays, NCCN reference, prior denial.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Prostate Cancer
American Society for Radiation Oncology (ASTRO) Clinical Practice Statement on IMRT

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of stereotactic body radiation therapy (CPT 77373) for our patient with three lung oligometastases from primary colon adenocarcinoma (ICD-10 C78.01). Per the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy and Stereotactic Radiosurgery, SBRT for pulmonary oligometastases is proven and medically necessary when the patient has KPS ≥70 or ECOG 0-2, ≤5 metastatic sites, controlled primary disease, and lesions meeting size limits. Submitted documentation: (1) ECOG performance status 1 documented by the treating medical oncologist on 2026-04-22; (2) three metastatic pulmonary nodules measuring 1.4 cm, 1.1 cm, and 0.9 cm on CT of 2026-05-08 (within 30 days of treatment plan, replacing the original 2025-12 imaging); (3) primary colon adenocarcinoma is in radiographic and CEA-marker remission since right hemicolectomy 2024-06 (CEA trend attached); (4) no extra-thoracic disease on PET/CT of 2026-05-09. The NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer V.5.2026 and the ASTRO Model Policy on SBRT support SBRT for pulmonary oligometastases meeting these criteria. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: updated CT and PET reports, ECOG documentation, CEA trend, colonoscopy surveillance report.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy and Stereotactic Radiosurgery
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer V.5.2026
American Society for Radiation Oncology (ASTRO) Model Policy on Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of proton beam therapy (CPT 77520) for our patient with an unresectable clival chordoma with cervical extension (ICD-10 C41.0 with C71.9 documentation in error). The original denial referenced adult glioma criteria. Corrected pathology: the lesion is a clival chordoma, brachyury-positive on IHC, biopsied 2026-03-15, NOT glioma. Per the UnitedHealthcare Oxford Clinical Policy: Proton Beam Radiation Therapy, chordomas and chondrosarcomas of the skull base are explicitly enumerated as covered indications. The NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Central Nervous System Cancers and the ASTRO Model Policy on Proton Beam Therapy list base-of-skull chordoma as a primary covered indication for proton therapy, citing the necessity of high-dose escalation adjacent to brainstem and optic apparatus. The IMRT alternative would exceed the brainstem D0.03cc constraint of 54 Gy (planned dose to PTV is 70 Gy). Submitted dosimetric comparison shows brainstem D0.03cc 62 Gy with IMRT vs 49 Gy with proton therapy. The denial was issued on the basis of an incorrect ICD-10 coding; the corrected pathology and Oxford policy text together establish coverage. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: corrected pathology with brachyury IHC, dosimetric comparison, neurosurgical resection note, MRI.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Oxford Clinical Policy: Proton Beam Radiation Therapy
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Central Nervous System Cancers
American Society for Radiation Oncology (ASTRO) Model Policy on Proton Beam Therapy

appeal_text: We request expedited first-level reconsideration of the denial of axicabtagene ciloleucel (Yescarta, CPT 0540T) for our patient with refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (ICD-10 C83.30). The denial cited absence of pre-procedure notification. We submit that the prior authorization request was filed via the UnitedHealthcare Provider Portal on 2026-04-12, twelve days before the scheduled apheresis on 2026-04-24; submission confirmation number is attached. Substantively, the patient meets all coverage criteria under the UnitedHealthcare Clinical Policy: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-Cell (CAR T) Therapy: (1) two prior lines of systemic therapy with documented refractory disease (R-CHOP 2025-01 through 2025-06 with primary refractory response on PET; R-DHAP 2025-08 through 2025-10 with progression on PET 2025-10-30); (2) ECOG 1, LVEF 60%, normal pulmonary and hepatic function; (3) treating center is an authorized REMS-certified Yescarta treatment center; (4) NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: B-Cell Lymphomas list axicabtagene ciloleucel as a Category 1 recommendation for relapsed/refractory DLBCL after two or more prior lines. The FDA prescribing information supports the indication on the basis of ZUMA-1. We request expedited review given the patient's rapidly progressive disease (LDH 845 U/L, doubling time approximately 5 weeks). Attached: portal submission confirmation, treatment-line documentation, REMS attestation, performance-status documentation, and the original denial.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Clinical Policy: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-Cell (CAR T) Therapy
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: B-Cell Lymphomas
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Yescarta (axicabtagene ciloleucel)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of reference bevacizumab (Avastin, CPT 96413) for our patient with metastatic colon adenocarcinoma (ICD-10 C18.9). The denial cited absent biosimilar step-therapy trial. The patient has documented intolerance to two preferred bevacizumab biosimilars satisfying the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage exception criteria: (1) bevacizumab-awwb (Mvasi) initiated 2025-08-04, discontinued 2025-09-22 after grade-3 hypertensive crisis (BP 198/112) on cycle 4 despite addition of amlodipine 10 mg daily — antihypertensive regimen documented and titrated; (2) bevacizumab-bvzr (Zirabev) initiated 2025-10-15, discontinued 2025-11-20 after grade-2 proteinuria (UPCR 3.4) and grade-3 epistaxis requiring nasal packing. The treating oncologist attests that recurrence of these adverse events is not expected with the reference product, consistent with patient-specific tolerability documented at the originator product. The current regimen is FOLFOX-bevacizumab per NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Colon Cancer V.4.2026 for first-line metastatic disease. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: medication-administration records, BP trend, urine protein trend, and chart notes documenting each adverse event.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Colon Cancer

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of daratumumab and hyaluronidase-fihj (Darzalex Faspro, CPT 96401) for our patient with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma (ICD-10 C90.00). The denial cited concurrent billing of a second anti-CD38 directed agent. Review of the patient's claim history identifies the duplicate billing as an administrative error by a separate infusion center on 2026-03-04 for a single isatuximab dose that was never administered; corrective claim reversal was filed 2026-03-11 (reversal confirmation attached). The intended regimen is daratumumab-CyBorD induction per NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Multiple Myeloma V.3.2026 (Category 1 for transplant-eligible newly diagnosed disease) consistent with the FDA-approved Darzalex Faspro indication. The patient is transplant-eligible (age 58, normal cardiac and pulmonary function). No active concurrent anti-CD38 therapy is administered. The UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage requires duplicative-therapy denial only when concurrent therapy is actually administered; the reversal documentation resolves the operational basis. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: claim reversal, infusion logs, NCCN reference, transplant evaluation note.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Multiple Myeloma

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of olaparib (Lynparza) maintenance therapy for our patient with metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma (ICD-10 C25.9). Subsequent to the denial we obtained germline genetic testing results: Invitae multi-gene panel of 2026-04-30 reports a pathogenic BRCA2 c.5946delT variant (frameshift, classified pathogenic per ClinVar). The patient has completed 5.2 months of first-line FOLFIRINOX (16 cycles) with stable disease on RECIST 1.1 (CT 2026-04-18). Per the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage and the NCCN Drugs & Biologics Compendium, olaparib first-line maintenance for metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma is covered when (1) the patient carries a deleterious or suspected-deleterious germline BRCA1/BRCA2 mutation and (2) has not progressed after ≥4 months of first-line platinum-based therapy. Both criteria are satisfied. The FDA prescribing information for Lynparza supports this indication on the basis of POLO. NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma V.2.2026 list olaparib as a Category 1 recommendation in this setting. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: germline NGS report, CT response assessment, FOLFIRINOX administration record, NCCN and FDA references.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Drugs & Biologics Compendium
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma
FDA-Approved Prescribing Information: Lynparza (olaparib)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of venetoclax (Venclexta) combination therapy for our patient. We note that the denial was based on an incorrect ICD-10 of C92.10 (chronic myeloid leukemia); the patient's actual diagnosis is acute myeloid leukemia (AML) with mutated IDH2 (ICD-10 C92.00), confirmed by bone marrow biopsy and NGS on 2026-02-22. The corrected diagnosis is supported by the attached corrected encounter form filed 2026-03-04. Per the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage and the NCCN Drugs & Biologics Compendium, venetoclax in combination with a hypomethylating agent (azacitidine) is a Category 1 recommendation for newly diagnosed AML in patients ineligible for intensive induction chemotherapy, consistent with NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Acute Myeloid Leukemia. The patient is 78 years old with ECOG 2 and HCT-CI score 5, formally meeting unfit-for-intensive-induction criteria per the treating hematologist. The FDA prescribing information for Venclexta supports this indication on the basis of VIALE-A. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: corrected ICD-10 documentation, bone marrow biopsy report, NGS report, geriatric assessment.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Drugs & Biologics Compendium
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Acute Myeloid Leukemia

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of nivolumab plus ipilimumab dual immunotherapy for our patient with metastatic cutaneous melanoma (ICD-10 C43.9). The denial cited missing biomarker documentation. Updated documentation: (1) BRAF V600E negative on Foundation One CDx; (2) PD-L1 IHC 22C3 reports CPS 12; (3) LDH 235 U/L (1.0x ULN); (4) no autoimmune contraindications; (5) ECOG 0. Per the UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage and the NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Melanoma: Cutaneous V.3.2026, the combination of nivolumab plus ipilimumab is a Category 1 preferred first-line regimen for BRAF-wild-type metastatic cutaneous melanoma irrespective of PD-L1 status. The FDA-approved labeling supports this indication on the basis of CheckMate 067. We additionally note that combination immunotherapy is the standard of care for patients with brain metastases (MRI 2026-04-09 demonstrates four supratentorial lesions of 4-7 mm) per CheckMate 204 data integrated into NCCN. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: NGS report, IHC report, brain MRI, autoimmune review of systems.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Melanoma: Cutaneous

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of FoundationOne CDx comprehensive genomic profiling (CPT 81455) for our patient with metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (ICD-10 C34.91). The denial cited that the panel is investigational. We submit that FoundationOne CDx is an FDA-approved companion diagnostic with a Category 1 NCCN recommendation. Per the NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer V.5.2026, broad molecular profiling at the time of metastatic diagnosis is a Category 1 recommendation; the guideline explicitly identifies EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, MET exon 14, RET, NTRK1/2/3, KRAS G12C, HER2, and PD-L1 as required biomarkers for first-line therapy selection. Single-gene sequential testing is documented to delay treatment initiation and exhaust tissue. The patient's prior FNA biopsy yielded limited tissue (cellblock with approximately 800 tumor cells); a NGS approach is required to preserve diagnostic tissue. The UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage explicitly relies on biomarker results (e.g., PD-L1 TPS ≥50% for first-line pembrolizumab) that cannot be obtained without comprehensive testing. CMS National Coverage Determination 90.2 covers FDA-approved NGS panels in advanced cancer. Submitted within the 65-day window. Attached: NCCN biomarker compendium, FDA approval letter for FoundationOne CDx, pathology report quantifying tumor cell yield, prior single-gene testing summary.
citations: UnitedHealthcare Commercial Medical Drug Policy: Oncology Medication Clinical Coverage
NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer V.5.2026
NCCN Biomarkers Compendium
